title: Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta Ad Photo-Asset Prep — Retouch, Grade, Cutout, Multi-Ratio reframe-crop & Delivery Pack for a West Palm Beach Mexican Restaurant
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real brief: family-owned authentic Mexican restaurant in West Palm Beach, FL; multicultural / strong Hispanic customer base; brand personality "authentic, community-focused, family-oriented, warm, bold, local neighborhood favorite," explicitly NOT a corporate chain; two named offers used here — "Taco Tuesday (3 concepts, drive Tuesday traffic, urgency, scroll-stop power)" and "Lunch Specials (3 concepts, drive lunch traffic)"; client supplies brand assets/logo, offer details, creative direction; "strong food photography" is the stated creative direction; deliverables are static feed/story ad MOCKUPS for client approval (not launched). ENRICHED to make it Adobe-connector-runnable: the original ask is "design 12 finished static ad layouts," which mixes typesetting/layout composition the Adobe connector cannot do headlessly (no text-to-image, no layout composition, no compositing). I therefore scope THIS benchmark to the photo-asset-preparation pipeline a designer genuinely does FIRST and hands to the layout stage — retouching and color-grading the hero food shots, building masked color-splash and subject-pop scroll-stop variants, cutting out a dish, reframe-cropping one hero into the three Meta ad ratios (1:1, 4:5, 9:16), licensing one stock backdrop texture, and vectorizing the supplied logo — then packaging everything as a labeled delivery set + Firefly board for client review. The specific dish names (al pastor tacos, carne asada lunch plate), the warm/bold grade direction, and the three ratio targets are reasonable enrichments consistent with the brief's creative direction; no unrelated deliverable was invented.
one_line_ask: Prep and deliver the campaign-ready photo + brand asset pack (retouched, warm/bold-graded hero food shots, color-splash and subject-pop scroll-stop variants, cut-out dishes, three Meta ad ratios via reframe-crop, a licensed backdrop texture, and a vectorized logo) for a West Palm Beach Mexican restaurant's Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta ad mockups.
full_brief:
You are the production designer prepping the photo and brand assets that will be dropped into 12 static Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ad mockups for a family-owned, authentic Mexican restaurant in West Palm Beach, FL. The brand is warm, bold, family-oriented and community-focused — a local neighborhood favorite, NOT a corporate chain. The campaign covers two offers: Taco Tuesday (scroll-stop, urgent, recurring-promo feel) and Lunch Specials. The client has supplied three raw phone/DSLR food photos (a hero al-pastor taco platter, a carne-asada lunch plate, a margarita-and-chips table scene), plus their logo as a flat PNG.

Your job, entirely within the Adobe connector:
1) Load the three supplied food photos and the logo PNG into the project.
2) Straighten and tonally fix the hero taco shot (auto-straighten the slightly tilted handheld frame, then auto-tone), so it reads clean and appetizing.
3) Push a warm, bold restaurant grade on the corrected hero: lift color temperature toward warm, boost vibrance/saturation so the salsa reds and lime greens pop, and recover highlight detail on the glossy meat — the "appetizing and bold" look the brand wants.
4) Build a scroll-stop "color-splash" variant from the graded hero: select the taco/food by prompt, invert the selection so everything BUT the food is selected, and desaturate that background so the food is the only color on screen (eye-catching scroll-stop power).
5) Apply an Adaptive "Subject Pop" preset to the graded hero to create a second high-contrast scroll-stop variant with the food made to stand forward.
6) Create a clean cut-out of the lunch plate: remove the background to a transparent PNG so the dish can float on any colored ad background the layout team chooses.
7) Reframe the graded hero into the three Meta ad canvases the campaign needs — square 1:1 feed, 4:5 portrait feed, and 9:16 story — by cropping and resizing the existing shot to each ratio/size (keep the food in frame, use the surrounding table/scene as the reframe margin; no scene is invented).
8) License one on-brand backdrop texture (warm woven Mexican textile / rustic wood) from Adobe Stock at full res for the layout team to use behind cut-outs.
9) Vectorize the supplied logo PNG into a clean SVG so it scales crisply across every ad size.
10) Assemble all produced assets into a Firefly board as a labeled review deck for the client, and copy the final delivery set into a clearly named project folder.

Deliverables: the corrected + warm-graded hero JPEG; the color-splash hero; the Subject-Pop hero; the transparent-PNG lunch-plate cut-out; three ratio-correct hero canvases (1:1, 4:5, 9:16); one licensed full-res backdrop texture; the logo SVG; a Firefly review board link; and a named delivery folder containing the set.

inputs.name: hero_taco_platter.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal candid overhead-ish handheld phone photo, slightly tilted ~4 degrees, of a generous al pastor taco platter on a rustic wooden table at a family-owned Mexican restaurant: three corn tortilla tacos piled with marinated pork, diced onion, cilantro, charred pineapple, lime wedges, small molcajete of red salsa, glossy meat catching warm window light. Real food texture, visible steam, natural slightly-mixed restaurant lighting, real grease sheen, no CGI gloss, documentary realism, minor crumbs on table.
inputs.realism_notes: Must be authentically imperfect: handheld tilt (so auto_straighten has work to do), slightly flat/cool phone-camera tone (so auto_tone + warm grade have work to do), real food specularity for highlight recovery. Avoid studio-perfect chain-restaurant look — brand is explicitly NOT corporate.

inputs.name: lunch_carne_asada_plate.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone photo of a carne asada lunch plate on a plain off-white restaurant plate against a moderately busy table background: grilled sliced steak, rice, refried beans, a folded warm tortilla, pico de gallo, lime. Clear subject/background separation so the dish can be cleanly masked. Warm natural daylight, real char marks, real steam, candid documentary realism, no CGI sheen.
inputs.realism_notes: Background should be a real cluttered table (other plates, condiments, blurred) so remove_background has a genuine non-trivial edge to cut — not a clean studio sweep.

inputs.name: margarita_chips_table.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal candid table scene at a warm neighborhood Mexican restaurant: a salt-rimmed margarita with lime, a basket of tortilla chips, a bowl of guacamole and a bowl of red salsa, on a rustic wood table with a woven textile runner, warm evening light, family-restaurant ambiance in the soft-blurred background. Real condensation on the glass, real chip texture, documentary realism, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Supporting scene shot; provides a third real food photo for the board and a candidate for additional grading. Mixed warm lighting and real condensation keep it honest.

inputs.name: restaurant_logo_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat 2D logo PNG on transparent background for a family-owned Mexican restaurant named 'El Vecino Cocina': bold hand-lettered wordmark with a small sombrero-and-chili-pepper emblem, warm terracotta-red and deep green palette, slightly hand-crafted neighborhood feel, crisp flat solid-color shapes (no gradients) suitable for vectorizing. Transparent background, high contrast.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat solid-color shapes with clean edges so image_vectorize produces a tidy SVG. Transparent PNG, modest resolution like a real client-supplied logo (e.g. 1200px wide), not a giant pristine vector export.

outputs.name: hero_warm_graded.jpg
outputs.spec: Straightened + auto-toned + warm/bold-graded hero taco platter; full-res JPEG, sRGB, the appetizing campaign hero.

outputs.name: hero_color_splash.jpg
outputs.spec: Scroll-stop variant: food in full color, background desaturated via inverted-mask saturation; full-res JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: hero_subject_pop.jpg
outputs.spec: Adaptive Subject-Pop preset applied to the graded hero; high-contrast scroll-stop variant; full-res JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: lunch_plate_cutout.png
outputs.spec: Carne asada lunch plate with background removed; transparent PNG, edges clean enough to float on any ad background.

outputs.name: hero_1x1_feed.jpg
outputs.spec: Hero reframed to square 1080x1080 (1:1) Meta feed canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 1:1 ratio; food kept in frame, surrounding table/scene trimmed as crop margin (no pixels invented); JPEG.

outputs.name: hero_4x5_feed.jpg
outputs.spec: Hero reframed to 1080x1350 (4:5) portrait feed canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 4:5 ratio; food kept in frame (no pixels invented); JPEG.

outputs.name: hero_9x16_story.jpg
outputs.spec: Hero reframed to 1080x1920 (9:16) story/Reels canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 9:16 ratio; food kept in frame (no pixels invented); JPEG.

outputs.name: backdrop_texture_fullres.jpg
outputs.spec: One licensed Adobe Stock warm woven-textile/rustic-wood backdrop texture, full-res, with license; for layout team to place behind cut-outs.

outputs.name: logo_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Clean SVG vectorization of the supplied logo PNG; scales crisply across all ad sizes.

outputs.name: ElVecino_MetaAd_AssetPack/
outputs.spec: Named project folder containing the full delivery set, plus a Firefly review board deep-link assembling all produced assets as a client-approval deck.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the four client-supplied files (3 food photos + logo PNG) into the project.
Finalize the upload; yields asset handles for hero_taco_platter.jpg, lunch_carne_asada_plate.jpg, margarita_chips_table.jpg, restaurant_logo_flat.png. These are the chain's root assets.
Straighten the ~4-degree handheld tilt on the hero taco platter (uses hero_taco_platter.jpg from loaded_assets).
Auto-tone the straightened hero for a clean balanced base before the creative grade.
Lift color temperature toward warm for the inviting neighborhood-restaurant feel.
Boost vibrance/saturation so salsa reds and lime greens pop (bold brand direction).
Recover blown highlight detail on the glossy meat — final appetizing campaign hero. PRIMARY graded deliverable.
Select the taco/food region by prompt on the graded hero (start of the color-splash variant).
Invert so everything BUT the food is selected.
Drive saturation down on the inverted (background) mask of the graded hero → color-splash scroll-stop variant. Food stays full color. DELIVERABLE.
Apply Adaptive 'Subject Pop' preset to the graded hero for a second high-contrast scroll-stop variant. DELIVERABLE.
Remove background on lunch_carne_asada_plate.jpg → transparent-PNG floating cut-out for flexible ad backgrounds. DELIVERABLE.

Reframe the graded hero to 1080x1080 (1:1) Meta feed by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the square ratio — keep the food in frame, trim surrounding table/scene margin (no pixels invented; gen-free reframe).
Reframe the graded hero to 1080x1350 (4:5) portrait feed by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 4:5 ratio — food kept in frame.
Reframe the graded hero to 1080x1920 (9:16) story/Reels by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the tall ratio — food kept in frame.
Search → license → download one full-res warm woven-textile/rustic-wood backdrop texture from Adobe Stock for layout backgrounds. (Stock search input is the campaign brief, not a prior step.)
Vectorize the flat logo PNG (restaurant_logo_flat.png from loaded_assets) into a clean scalable SVG. DELIVERABLE.
Create the named delivery folder 'ElVecino_MetaAd_AssetPack' to hold the final set.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: [delivery_folder, hero_warm_graded.jpg, hero_color_splash.jpg, hero_subject_pop.jpg, lunch_plate_cutout.png, hero_1x1_feed.jpg, hero_4x5_feed.jpg, hero_9x16_story.jpg, backdrop_texture_fullres.jpg, logo_vector.svg]
connector_workflow.note: Copy all produced deliverables into the named delivery folder.
Assemble the copied delivery set into a Firefly review board (deep-link) as the client-approval deck. FINAL deliverable bundle.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-2 load the four client assets (loaded_assets is the only root that doesn't consume a prior step, plus the two pure search/create steps 16 and 18 whose input is the brief). The hero pipeline is fully linear: loaded_assets → auto_straighten (hero_straight) → auto_tone (hero_autotone) → color_temperature (hero_warm) → vibrance (hero_vibrant) → highlights (hero_warm_graded.jpg). hero_warm_graded.jpg is then the shared parent of FIVE downstream branches: (a) color-splash = select_by_prompt(food_mask) → invert_selection(background_mask) → vibrance-on-mask consuming BOTH hero_warm_graded.jpg + background_mask; (b) apply_preset → subject_pop; (c-e) three image_crop_and_resize reframes of the graded hero to the 1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16 Meta ad ratios (crop/resample existing pixels to each target size — no pixels invented). lunch_plate_cutout and logo_vector each branch off the original loaded_assets (different source files). Finally asset_create_folders → asset_copy_assets (consumes the folder + all nine deliverables) → create_firefly_board (consumes the copied delivery_set). Every non-root step lists its producing step(s) in inputs_from; the final board emerges from the assembled chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 20 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (A1 raster/photo, A1 vector, A4 asset/stock/board, plus upload/storage). All steps are [C] headless-confirmed — no [W]/[A]/[T]/[X] dependencies, so it runs autonomously on Adobe alone. The chain is genuinely deep (a 6-step linear grade feeding a 5-way branch) and reuses the graded hero many times, exercising masked selective editing (select_by_prompt → invert → masked saturation for color-splash), an Adaptive preset, background removal, the sole reframe/crop step (image_crop_and_resize, which crops/resamples existing pixels only and invents none) across three real Meta ad ratios, stock licensing, vectorization, and a folder+board delivery package. It deliberately stays inside the constraints: NO layout composition (the 12 finished mockups' typesetting is explicitly out of scope and handed off), NO text-to-image, NO compositing, NO bg-replace-by-prompt, image_fill_area is not abused (saturation, not fill, does the desaturate), and the single local-packaging escape hatch is not needed (folder+board are real Adobe asset tools). Grounded faithfully in the real Upwork restaurant brief.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned generative capability remains. The three ratio steps (n=13/14/15) use image_crop_and_resize, which only crops/downsamples existing pixels to the target ratio and size — it invents no pixels and never grows the source beyond its own bounds. No text-to-image, no generative expand/outpaint, no upscale/super-resolution, no from-scratch art, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no generative background replace. image_remove_background (n=12) is a plain masked cutout to transparency. The color-splash desaturate (n=10) is a masked tonal adjust, not a fill; image_fill_area is not used. image_apply_preset (n=11) applies a stock Adaptive preset. asset_license_and_download_stock (n=16) licenses existing Adobe Stock. All 20 steps are server=='adobe' and exec_mode 'C' (headless). Every input a step consumes is declared in inputs[] (three food photos + logo PNG).
reverify.notes: Reconciled stale pre-fix wording: rewrote the outputs[] specs for hero_1x1_feed / hero_4x5_feed / hero_9x16_story (removed 'reframe-cropped / scene extended') to describe the actual crop/resize reframe, and rewrote the chaining_note clause '(c-e) three generative-expand outpaints' to 'three image_crop_and_resize reframes'. No workflow, deliverable intent, or target dimensions changed.
